trans-unit.source: (optional — to follow up on your feedback)
Please enter a valid email address.
Your feedback helps us make MotionHive better for everyone.
e.g. Session attendance not saving correctly
What were you doing when it happened? What did you expect vs. what actually occurred?
e.g. Show progress charts per client
Describe the feature and how it would improve your workflow as a trainer or organizer...
e.g. Question about managing group sessions
What's on your mind? We're happy to help.
Could not send feedback. Please try again.
Be the first to know when MotionHive launches
We're building something special. Drop your email and we'll notify you the moment we're live.
Please enter a valid email address.
We'll reach out as soon as MotionHive is ready. Thanks for believing in what we're building.
Could not join the waitlist. Please try again.
Built for people who show up.
MotionHive exists because staying active together shouldn't require five different apps.

If you've ever organised a group activity — a class, a run, a weekly session — you know how it goes. WhatsApp for scheduling, spreadsheets for attendance, Instagram for updates, and half the group still out of the loop.
There's no single place where the person leading and the people showing up can just stay in sync. No noise, no switching between apps, no chaos.
That's what MotionHive is. One platform for active communities — whether you're running a yoga class, a hiking group, a martial arts club, or anything that gets people moving together.
It's early and we're building it in the open. No inflated numbers, no empty promises. Just the thing that should've existed a long time ago.
A few principles that shape everything we build.
The best motivation isn't an app notification — it's the people you show up with.
If it takes more than a minute to figure out, we built it wrong.
Yoga, hiking, martial arts, dance — if people come together to move, it belongs here.
No fake metrics. We share what's ready, what's next, and what we're learning along the way.
We write about what we're building, the decisions we make, and what's coming next.
MotionHive — Where active communities come together
We're building MotionHive in the open. Check out more stories from the hive.
What we're building, why we're building it, and everything we learn along the way.
Try adjusting your search or filter to find what you're looking for.
Want to stay in the loop?
We're building MotionHive in the open. Follow along and be the first to know when the hive opens.
Make staying active together feel easy.
Whether you lead a group or just love showing up — this is your space to stay active, stay connected, and find your people.
Free to organise. Free to join. Always. — we're building something good.
One place to schedule sessions, manage members, and stay organised.
Browse local fitness groups and book your spot in one tap.
Run sessions and classes on your terms.
Keep your community in one place.
Stay connected to your favourite groups.
Browse sessions nearby and jump in.
We're building MotionHive piece by piece. Here's what's landing first.
MotionHive is just getting started. We're building the hive — follow along on our blog and be the first to know when it opens.
Create your space, invite your people, and organise everything in one place.
Plan activities, set recurring sessions, and let people join with one tap.
Share stories, tips, and news to keep your community in the loop.
Your public profile, upcoming sessions, and activity history — all in one place.
Where active communities come together. One place to lead, join, and stay moving.

At MotionHive, we take your privacy seriously. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, disclose, and safeguard your information when you use our Service. Please read this policy carefully to understand our practices regarding your personal data.
We may collect the following personal information:
Name and contact information (email, phone number)
Payment information (processed securely by third-party payment processors)
Device information (IP address, browser type, operating system)
Usage data (pages visited, features used, time spent)
Health metrics (if you choose to provide them)
Provide, maintain, and improve our Service
Process your transactions and manage your account
Send you updates, newsletters, and marketing communications (with your consent)
Respond to your inquiries and provide customer support
Personalize your experience and deliver relevant content
Monitor and analyze usage patterns and trends
Detect, prevent, and address technical issues and security threats
We do not sell your personal information. We may share your information with:
Service Providers: Third parties who help us operate our Service
Business Partners: With your consent, for joint offerings
Legal Requirements: When required by law or to protect our rights
Business Transfers: In connection with a merger, acquisition, or sale of assets
We implement appropriate technical and organizational measures to protect your personal information against unauthorized access, alteration, disclosure, or destruction. These measures include:
Encryption of data in transit and at rest
Access: Request a copy of your personal information
Correction: Update or correct inaccurate information
Deletion: Request deletion of your personal information
Portability: Receive your data in a machine-readable format
Object: Object to certain processing of your data

We use cookies and similar tracking technologies to improve your experience. You can control cookies through your browser settings. However, disabling cookies may affect certain features of our Service.

Our Service is not intended for children under 18 years of age. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children. If you believe we have collected information from a child, please contact us immediately.

Your information may be transferred to and maintained on servers located outside your jurisdiction. By using our Service, you consent to such transfers. We ensure appropriate safeguards are in place to protect your data.

We retain your personal information for as long as necessary to fulfill the purposes outlined in this Privacy Policy, unless a longer retention period is required by law. When we no longer need your information, we will securely delete or anonymize it.

We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. We will notify you of material changes by posting the new Privacy Policy on this page and updating the "Last Updated" date. We encourage you to review this policy periodically.
If you have questions or concerns about this Privacy Policy, please contact us:
Also read our Terms of Service

Welcome to MotionHive. These Terms of Service ("Terms") govern your access to and use of our website, mobile application, and services (collectively, the "Service"). By accessing or using the Service, you agree to be bound by these Terms.
If you do not agree to these Terms, please do not use our Service.

By creating an account or using MotionHive, you acknowledge that you have read, understood, and agree to be bound by these Terms and our Privacy Policy. If you are using the Service on behalf of an organization, you represent that you have the authority to bind that organization to these Terms.
You must be at least 18 years old to create an account
You must provide accurate and complete information during registration
You are responsible for maintaining the security of your account credentials
You must notify us immediately of any unauthorized access to your account
You are responsible for all activities that occur under your account
Use the Service only for lawful purposes
Not interfere with or disrupt the Service or servers
Not attempt to gain unauthorized access to any part of the Service
Not transmit viruses, malware, or other malicious code
Not impersonate any person or entity
Respect the intellectual property rights of MotionHive and others

While we strive to provide continuous access to the Service, we do not guarantee that the Service will be available at all times. We may suspend, withdraw, or restrict the availability of all or any part of the Service for business or operational reasons.
Some features of the Service may require a paid subscription:
Subscription fees are billed in advance on a recurring basis
All fees are non-refundable unless otherwise stated
You can cancel your subscription at any time
We reserve the right to change our pricing with 30 days notice

The Service and its original content, features, and functionality are owned by MotionHive and are protected by international copyright, trademark, patent, trade secret, and other intellectual property laws.

We may terminate or suspend your account and access to the Service immediately, without prior notice or liability, for any reason, including breach of these Terms. Upon termination, your right to use the Service will immediately cease.

To the maximum extent permitted by law, MotionHive shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages, or any loss of profits or revenues, whether incurred directly or indirectly, or any loss of data, use, goodwill, or other intangible losses.

We reserve the right to modify these Terms at any time. We will notify users of any material changes by posting the new Terms on this page and updating the "Last Updated" date. Your continued use of the Service after such changes constitutes acceptance of the new Terms.
If you have any questions about these Terms, please contact us:
Powered by the Mifflin-St Jeor equation.
Calculate your daily calorie needs based on your personal information and activity level.
Enter valid measurements to see your body fat estimate.
35 ml per kg of body weight
Master your metabolism and macronutrient ratios.

Total Daily Energy Expenditure (TDEE) represents the cumulative calories your body burns in a 24-hour period. For active individuals, TDEE varies significantly depending on daily exertion levels. While the Mifflin-St Jeor equation serves as the primary benchmark for estimation, you should refine your target by 100–200 kcal after two weeks based on real-world weight data.

Hitting your calorie target is the first lever — but the macro split determines whether those calories rebuild muscle, replenish glycogen, or support hormonal balance. A 40 / 30 / 30 distribution (carbs / protein / fat) works well for most group fitness athletes. Adjust towards more protein (up to 35–40%) if your primary goal is body recomposition.
Calculate your Total Daily Energy Expenditure (TDEE) and macros for free. Built for group fitness athletes using the Mifflin-St Jeor equation.
TDEE calculator, calorie calculator, macro calculator, body fat calculator, group fitness, weight loss, bulking, Mifflin-St Jeor
Find your maintenance calories, macro split, and body fat percentage — free, instant, no sign-up required.
How accurate is this calorie calculator?

This calculator uses the Mifflin-St Jeor equation, which is considered the most accurate formula for estimating Basal Metabolic Rate (BMR) for most people. Studies show it has a margin of error of roughly ±10%. For greater precision, consider a DEXA scan or a clinical resting metabolic rate test.
Should I eat more on HIIT days?

Yes — high-intensity interval training significantly elevates calorie expenditure. On hard training days, you may benefit from an extra 200–400 calories. Prioritise complex carbohydrates before your session and protein-rich foods within 30 minutes of finishing to support performance and recovery.
What is a safe calorie deficit for weight loss?

A deficit of 300–500 calories per day is generally considered safe and sustainable, leading to roughly 0.3–0.5 kg (0.5–1 lb) of fat loss per week. Deficits exceeding 1,000 calories per day risk muscle loss, nutrient deficiencies, and metabolic adaptation that can stall progress.
